Corvin Studio

Mix & Mastering for Extreme & Esoteric Music

Main

For years, the work remained veiled — formed through discipline and silent recognition, without name or announcement. Corvin Studio marks the moment of opening, where entry is now permitted into what was once found only by those called to it.

About

History

For years, the work remained veiled — formed through discipline and silent recognition, without name or announcement. Corvin Studio marks the moment of opening, where entry is now permitted into what was once found only by those called to it.

What exists now did not begin as a plan, but as a gradual alignment — a process of becoming.

In the early stages, the work took form through limitation. A modest four-track recorder became the first instrument of translation — a primitive device through which intention was captured, tested, and refined. What began as imitation soon revealed something else: a fixation on tone, density, and atmosphere that extended beyond simple recording. Even then, the process was less technical than devotional — an attempt to channel something that could not yet be fully articulated.

As the work deepened, it began to draw others.

Early recordings — raw, unpolished, but carrying a certain charge — began circulating. What started as isolated experimentation became contact. Bands arrived, not through promotion, but through recognition. There was an understanding, however unspoken, that the space was not neutral — that something particular was being cultivated within it. Limitations dissolved in stages. Equipment expanded. Capability followed. But the core remained unchanged: a commitment to a specific current of sound — one rooted in extremity, atmosphere, and intent.

Eventually, the work required separation from the ordinary.

Spaces were sought not for convenience, but for their character — enclosed environments, removed from distraction, where time behaved differently. Long hours blurred into continuous sessions. Sound was not simply recorded, but shaped, layered, and invoked. Many of the early works that emerged during this period now sit as formative documents within their respective scenes — not through scale, but through permanence.

As years passed, the output grew. What began as demos shifted into full-length works, each one reinforcing a distinct identity. Artists returned. Others followed. A pattern established itself — not of expansion for its own sake, but of consolidation. The work began to speak for itself.

Relocation, when it occurred, was never accidental.

Each transition marked a shift in scale and intent. New spaces were acquired and reconfigured, always with the same objective: to build an environment where sound and atmosphere could reach their full extent. The current adapted, but never diluted.

This path has now led to Glastonbury, Somerset.

Here, the work has taken root within a landscape already saturated with centuries of symbolism, ritual, and layered meaning. The studio exists within a structure reclaimed and repurposed — a space once aligned to one form of devotion, now transformed into another. High ceilings, natural resonance, and physical stillness provide an environment where sound expands without constraint.

The location is not incidental.

Glastonbury has long functioned as a point of convergence — a place where myth, history, and esoteric tradition overlap. The presence of the Tor, the waters of the Chalice Well, and the crossing of the Michael and Mary currents have drawn seekers, writers, and practitioners for generations. Figures such as Geoffrey of Monmouth and later mystics and occult thinkers helped cement its reputation as a threshold space — a place of burial, of transformation, of return.

The work now continues within this context.

While the foundation remains in extreme music, the scope has evolved where necessary — allowing certain projects to move outward while maintaining the same underlying discipline. Production, mixing, and mastering have all become extensions of the same philosophy: to create work that endures, that carries weight, and that aligns with everything that has come before it. In parallel, the process has become less bound to location. Some are brought into the space. Others are worked with remotely, or encountered in their own environments. The method adapts — the standard does not.

Nothing here is accidental.

Those who enter into the process become part of a continuum — a body of work shaped over time, defined not by volume, but by consistency and intent.

The path remains open — but not to everyone.

Richard Corvinus
Founder

Location

Established in Glastonbury — a locus of convergence and enduring symbolic weight — Corvin Studio operates as an extension of its surroundings. The space, the land, and the work are aligned; what is created here is inseparable from where it is formed.

Production

Production, Mixing
& Mastering

We accept only a select few projects, shaping production, mixing, and mastering into work that can stand within our catalogue—aligned, sealed, and enduring.

Pricing follows the demands of the material (with services like drum editing, reamping, and restoration folded in where needed), and once the scope is set, we hold it with discipline. If you’re ready to commit, make contact.

Price tailored to the specific project upon discussion.

Equipment

A high-end hybrid
workflow

Corvin Studio is built around a high-end hybrid workflow: an M3 Ultra Mac Studio running Logic Pro and industry-standard restoration/mastering tools, paired with SSL control and premium conversion (Apogee Symphony I/O MkII and SSL AD/DA) for precision capture and recall.

Monitoring is reference-grade and translation-focused, spanning Genelec mains, multiple Neumann nearfields, and classic-style checks (Avantone) for mixes that hold up everywhere.

On the recording side, the studio is equipped for extreme tones and full-band tracking—serious bass and guitar amplification, extensive preamp options, boutique dynamics/EQ, a deep mic locker (including ribbons and large-diaphragm condensers), triggers, and a Paiste RUDE / Pearl drum setup designed for big, controlled, thunderous sessions.

DAW & Computer

  • M3 Ultra Mac Studio
  • Logic Pro
  • Industry-standard restoration / mastering tools

Conversion & Control

  • SSL control surface
  • Apogee Symphony I/O MkII
  • SSL AD/DA conversion

Monitoring

  • Genelec mains
  • Neumann nearfields (multiple)
  • Avantone — classic-style checks

Amplification

  • Marshall JVM410H
  • Blackstar Series One
  • Orange Cabinet

Drums

  • Pearl drum setup
  • Paiste RUDE cymbals
  • Triggers

Tracking

  • Deep mic locker — ribbons, large-diaphragm condensers
  • Extensive preamp options
  • Boutique dynamics / EQ
